intro.text: Concept & Interior Design, Brand Experience, Customer Journey, Menu & Experience Design

content.text: Designing Identity into Dining

ANA was conceived as a living cultural canvas, reflecting the UAE’s diversity and Emirati hospitality. The façade features a “no-logo logo” wall, where every version of “ANA / أنا” represents a different personality and identity. This symbolism mirrors the UAE as a melting pot of cultures, translated into Emirati-fusion cuisine, interior design, and a holistic guest experience. The design balances contemporary Arabesque aesthetics with modern luxury — refined materials, warm tones, and bold art statements. From dining areas to the majlis event zone, every detail reinforces ANA’s philosophy of celebrating individuality through shared experience.
When Fragrance Becomes Art

At Abu Dhabi Art Fair, Hind Al-Oud revealed Ahojas through an immersive installation that transformed fragrance into art. Designed as a sculptural, multi-sensory environment, the space invited visitors to think, feel, and create, positioning perfume as both an emotional journey and a contemporary artwork.

intro.text: Flagship Store | Luxury Retail Architecture | Rebranding Rollout

content.text: Designing the Future of Emirati Perfumery

The Anfasic flagship at Dubai Mall reimagines traditional perfume retail into a futuristic, immersive environment. Curved forms, glowing violet tones, and reflective surfaces create a space that feels both rooted in Emirati culture and ahead of its time. This store became the first physical expression of Anfasic’s rebranding, a bold step into modern luxury design within one of the world’s most prestigious retail destinations.
Framing Watches as Works of Art

The Charles Zuber boutique at Yas Mall marks the brand’s first global flagship outside Switzerland. Designed at the Geneva headquarters, the Abu Dhabi boutique was envisioned as a contemporary temple of watchmaking and jewelry, blending Swiss precision with a Middle Eastern sense of luxury.
Futuristic Auditorium & Training Hub

For Canadian University Dubai, a new auditorium and innovation hub was designed to serve as both the university’s formal ceremonial hall and a training ground for the next generation of marketers and brand builders. With a capacity of 200–300, the space embodies a futuristic, VIP atmosphere aligned with the university’s innovative spirit.

intro.text: Experiential Pop-Up | Artistic Installation | Brand Activation

content.text: When Scent Becomes Art

At Dubai Design Week, Hind Al-Oud presented an immersive pop-up installation that transformed fragrance into art and poetry into space. Inspired by Kibriya’E by poet Faisal Al-Adwani, the activation introduced two exclusive perfumes — Alifi and Ya’E — expressed as a multi-sensory journey from certainty to singularity, blending scent, light, sound, and spatial storytelling into an evocative design experience.

data.location: Sharjah Research Technology and Innovation Park, UAE

content.text: Emotional Design for Creativity

The Emovation Lab was designed as a first-of-its-kind hub for emotional innovation in Sharjah’s Technology and Innovation Park. Created as a space where invention begins with emotion, it offered a multi-sensory environment for students, researchers, and creators to imagine, develop, and showcase new ideas.
The Architecture of Emotion

The Emovation Lab was a pioneering multi-sensory pop-up, born from the fusion of emotion and innovation. It transformed fragrance into an immersive journey, redefining how customers engage with luxury retail.

intro.text: Department Store – Retail Architecture & Multi-Brand Concept

content.text: The First Emirati Luxury Department Store Reimagined

HOB marked the UAE’s first Emirati luxury department store, and this Yas Mall flagship was a pivotal moment in elevating its identity. The store was envisioned as a premium multi-brand destination — housing perfumes, fashion, accessories, jewelry, and private event spaces — designed to reflect global luxury standards while remaining rooted in regional identity.
Design Concept
This immersive pop-up was designed as a luxury pavilion in the heart of Galleria Mall, combining bold architecture with sensorial storytelling to create a memorable retail destination.
The First Flagship of a Global Rollout

The Hind Al-Oud Bas Mall flagship marked the beginning of a full rebranding journey, setting a new benchmark for luxury perfume retail. Designed as an immersive environment where architecture, scent, and storytelling converge, it became the first store in a global rollout that now extends across the UAE, GCC, Moscow, and London.

intro.text: Corporate Interior | Conference Hall | Experiential Design

content.text: Futuristic Minimalism with Luxury Detail

The Innovation Conference Hall was conceived as a prestigious space for VIP gatherings, strategic meetings, and high-level discussions. Designed with a futuristic minimalist language and luxurious detailing, the hall embodies authority, sophistication, and forward-looking vision, creating an environment where leadership and innovation converge.

intro.text: Corporate Headquarters | Experiential Office Design | Emotional Spatial Concept

content.text: Where Work Becomes Wellbeing

The MHG Headquarters was envisioned as more than a workplace — it was designed as an emotional journey. I led the project from concept to completion, shaping the headquarters around the philosophy of Breath Out, Breath In, Innovation. Each zone was crafted to influence mood, wellbeing, and creativity, turning the office into a living ecosystem of energy and belonging.

intro.text: Private CEO Office | Perfume Lab | Luxury Interior Design

content.text: Architecting Calm & Authority

This CEO office was designed as both an executive headquarters and a creative laboratory, reflecting the dual role of a leader who drives a luxury retail group while personally creating fragrances. The space delivers a first impression of prestige and refinement, while offering a calm, home-like atmosphere rarely found in corporate environments.
A Journey Through Emotions

“The Pink Emotions” campaign was launched during Breast Cancer Awareness Month, reimagining awareness as an emotional journey. Instead of relying on symbolic pink visuals alone, the concept translated the stages of a woman’s healing process — discovery, empowerment, healing, and renewal — into a multi-sensory narrative across digital, retail, and live experiences.
